title: This Planet Spins Backwards And We Still Don't Fully Know Why
hook: On Venus, the sun rises in the west. Every other major planet spins the same direction it orbits. Venus does the opposite.
body: A day on Venus is also longer than its entire year, which by itself makes it the strangest clock in the solar system. The leading theory is an ancient, massive collision early in the planet's history that flipped or drastically slowed its spin. But some researchers argue the real cause might not be a single dramatic impact at all, but a slow tug of war between the thick Venusian atmosphere and the sun's gravity over billions of years, gradually braking and reversing the spin. Both ideas are still on the table because Venus's surface is hidden under clouds of sulfuric acid, so we can't just look for an obvious scar from an impact.
cta: We've sent more landers to Venus than almost anywhere else, and it still keeps this secret. Follow for the next space mystery.
description: Why does Venus rotate backwards compared to almost every other planet? The two leading theories, explained fast.
